project.name: Facility Overseer
project.description: A Windows WPF desktop application for managing Abiotic Factor dedicated game servers — installs the server via SteamCMD, configures gameplay settings through a schema-driven UI, and monitors runtime health across multiple world instances.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: AI agent routing manifest for the Facility Overseer project, listing sub-agents by workstream, enforcing house-rule skills, defining phase ordering, and providing project build/test commands.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive user-facing project overview covering features, installation steps, file layout, troubleshooting guidance, and a developer quick-start for the Facility Overseer Windows GUI.

nodes.name: global.json
nodes.summary: Global .NET SDK version pin for the solution, ensuring all developers and CI agents use a consistent SDK toolchain.

nodes.name: CURRENT_BUILD.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive architecture reference for Facility Overseer, covering the four-project solution layout, end-to-end server generation pipeline, runtime health/roster/logging subsystems, the dynamic sandbox schema engine, UI window/tab structure, known limitations, and proposed RCON seam — intended as ground truth for AI agents planning refactors.

nodes.name: MASTER_PLAN.md
nodes.summary: Single source-of-truth master plan for the Facility Overseer refactor, tracking all tasks across three phases (Stability & Identity, Operational Intelligence, Remote Admin/RCON) with checkbox status, acceptance gates, sub-agent ownership table, skill house-rules, test matrix, and open questions.

nodes.name: UI_TWEAKS_PLAN.md
nodes.summary: Focused UI-only cleanup plan captured from the 2026-05-21 live review, organizing 30+ discrete tweaks across five groups (title card, networking tab, diagnostics, settings tabs, logs/status) with state tracking, locked design decisions, suggested implementation sequence, and test/verification checklist.

nodes.name: .gitattributes
nodes.summary: Git attributes configuration that sets line-ending normalization rules for the repository.

nodes.name: AbioticServerManager.slnx

nodes.name: Directory.Build.props
nodes.summary: MSBuild directory-level props file that applies shared compiler settings (nullable, warnings-as-errors, target framework) to all projects in the repository.
nodes.languageNotes: Directory.Build.props is imported automatically by MSBuild for every project under the directory, making it the ideal place to enforce repo-wide compile settings like TreatWarningsAsErrors.

nodes.name: Directory.Packages.props
nodes.summary: Central Package Management file that pins NuGet package versions for all projects, preventing version drift across the solution.
nodes.languageNotes: With Central Package Management enabled, individual csproj files reference packages without version numbers; the versions are authoritative here.

nodes.name: AbioticServerManager.App.csproj

nodes.name: app.manifest

nodes.languageNotes: In WPF, App.xaml acts as the application-level resource host; merged dictionaries here are available globally to all windows and controls.

nodes.name: App

nodes.name: OnStartup
nodes.summary: Runs on WPF startup: presents the data-root picker if needed, initializes the Generic Host with all services, shows the MainWindow, then fires InitializeAsync to load world state.

nodes.name: AssemblyInfo.cs
nodes.summary: Assembly-level attributes file declaring WPF theme and resource dictionary merging behavior for the application.

nodes.name: AutoScrollBehavior.cs

nodes.name: PasswordBoxBehavior.cs

nodes.name: MainWindow.xaml.cs

nodes.name: SandboxCategoryPanel.xaml.cs

nodes.name: SandboxCategoryPanel

nodes.name: ClipboardHelper.cs

nodes.languageNotes: At 919 lines this is the central style sheet for the app, defining named brushes and control templates used throughout all windows.

nodes.name: MainViewModel.cs
nodes.summary: Primary application ViewModel (3254 lines) orchestrating all server management operations: world CRUD, server start/stop/install, sandbox settings, network diagnostics, admin/ban management, backup lifecycle, roster tracking, and log tailing.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses CommunityToolkit.Mvvm ObservableObject with partial property notation; dispatches UI updates via WPF Dispatcher for background-thread coroutines.

nodes.name: MainViewModel
nodes.summary: Massive ObservableObject ViewModel injected with 20+ Core and Infrastructure services; owns all command bindings, async operations, and UI state for the main application window.

nodes.name: InitializeAsync

nodes.name: StartServer

nodes.name: InstallOrUpdateServer

nodes.name: CreateFirewallRules

nodes.name: ApplyNetworkSetupStatus

nodes.name: BanRowAsync

nodes.name: SaveAsync

nodes.name: NotificationChipMarkers.cs
nodes.summary: Small constants or enum file defining notification chip marker identifiers used in the UI to display contextual status chips on server worlds.

nodes.name: RosterRowViewModel.cs

nodes.name: RosterRowViewModel

nodes.name: SandboxCategoryViewModel.cs

nodes.name: SandboxCategoryViewModel

nodes.name: SandboxSettingsViewModel.cs
nodes.summary: ViewModel managing the full sandbox INI editor surface: loads the settings document into categorized SandboxCategoryViewModels, handles edits, saves, revert-from-file, and bucket-reset operations.

nodes.name: SandboxSettingsViewModel
nodes.summary: Orchestrates sandbox settings presentation: groups raw settings into categories, orders them by rank, provides edit/reset/revert commands, and delegates persistence to the sandbox service.

nodes.name: LoadFrom

nodes.name: ServerInstanceViewModel.cs
nodes.summary: The central view model for a single server instance, coordinating health tracking, player roster management, moderation lists, network diagnostics, backup state, and startup phase display for the WPF UI.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses CommunityToolkit.Mvvm observable properties; the class exposes over 60 bindable properties covering every tab of the server UI panel.

nodes.name: ServerInstanceViewModel
nodes.summary: Observable view model that drives the per-server tab UI, aggregating health state, player activity, roster, network checks, admin/ban lists, backups, and sandbox settings into a single bindable surface.

nodes.name: ApplyHealth
nodes.summary: Processes a server log line through the health state machine, updates startup phases, extracts the lobby code, and triggers online corroboration when the server is considered reachable.

nodes.name: RefreshRoster
nodes.summary: Rebuilds the Roster and BannedPlayers observable collections from the underlying roster model, applying admin and ban set filters and preserving the currently-selected player detail view.

nodes.name: ApplyPlayerActivity
nodes.summary: Applies a log line to the player activity tracker and synchronizes the ActivePlayers, PlayerActivityHistory, and PlayerSessions observable collections.

nodes.name: ConfirmOnlineFromCorroboration
nodes.summary: Elevates the server health state to confirmed-online when corroborating evidence (A2S, lobby code, player join) is observed, refreshes startup phases, and pushes updated health to the UI.

nodes.name: UpdateModerationLists
nodes.summary: Accepts fresh moderator and banned-player ID sets from the admin INI and triggers a roster refresh to reflect the updated permission state.

nodes.name: ServerLogEntry.cs
nodes.summary: Simple record or data class representing a single timestamped log line for display in the server log tab.

nodes.name: SettingViewModel.cs
nodes.summary: View model for an individual sandbox or server configuration setting, wrapping a descriptor with typed value accessors (bool, double, string, option) and a commit callback that propagates changes back to the INI layer.

nodes.name: SettingViewModel
nodes.summary: Bindable wrapper around a setting descriptor that exposes typed value properties and a Commit method, enabling heterogeneous setting controls to share a single observable model.

nodes.name: Commit
nodes.summary: Applies a new canonical string value to the setting descriptor, invokes the write-back callback, and fires change notifications for all derived display properties.

nodes.name: CreateWorldDialog.xaml.cs

nodes.name: CreateWorldDialog

nodes.name: DataRootPickerWindow.xaml

nodes.name: DataRootPickerWindow.xaml.cs
nodes.summary: Code-behind for the data-root picker that browses for a folder, validates write access by creating a probe file, and returns the chosen absolute path to the application startup flow.

nodes.name: DataRootPickerWindow

nodes.name: OnContinueClicked
nodes.summary: Validates the entered or browsed path for non-emptiness, optionally creates the directory, checks write access, then stores the canonical full path and closes the window.

nodes.name: LegacyDataImportDialog.xaml

nodes.name: LegacyDataImportDialog.xaml.cs

nodes.name: RecommendedActionsWindow.xaml

nodes.name: RecommendedActionsWindow.xaml.cs

nodes.name: SettingTemplateSelector.cs
nodes.summary: WPF DataTemplateSelector that picks among Toggle, Slider, Number, Dropdown, and Text templates based on the control type of a SettingViewModel, enabling heterogeneous setting controls in a single ItemsControl.

nodes.summary: XAML markup for the world integrity findings window that displays file-system and configuration issues detected for a world.

nodes.name: WorldIntegrityWindow.xaml.cs
nodes.summary: Minimal code-behind for the world integrity window, wiring the world view model as DataContext on construction.

nodes.name: AbioticServerManager.Core.csproj
nodes.summary: MSBuild project file for the Core class library, defining the target framework, nullable/warnings-as-errors settings, and NuGet package references for the platform-agnostic server management domain layer.

nodes.name: AdminIniBanEditor.cs
nodes.summary: Pure static class that parses, adds, and removes player ban entries from the [BannedPlayers] section of the admin INI text, preserving section headers and comments through all mutations.

nodes.name: AdminIniBanEditor
nodes.summary: Static editor for the [BannedPlayers] INI section offering ListBans, IsBanned, AddBan, and RemoveBan operations that work purely on the text string, with no file I/O.
nodes.languageNotes: All methods are pure text-transform functions following the core-pure-discipline skill: no IO, returns new string.

nodes.name: ListBans
nodes.summary: Parses the [BannedPlayers] section of an admin INI string and returns the list of raw Steam ID values found under the configured key prefix.

nodes.name: AddBan
nodes.summary: Inserts a new ban entry into the [BannedPlayers] section of the INI text, creating the section if absent and skipping if the ID is already banned.

nodes.name: RemoveBan
nodes.summary: Removes a specific player's ban entry from the [BannedPlayers] section of the INI text, leaving other sections and comments intact.

nodes.name: AdminIniModeratorEditor.cs
nodes.summary: Pure static class that manages [Moderators] entries in the admin INI text, supporting add, remove, and bulk-replace operations while preserving all other sections and comments.

nodes.name: AdminIniModeratorEditor
nodes.summary: Static editor for the [Moderators] INI section with ListModerators, IsModerator, AddModerator, RemoveModerator, and ReplaceModerators operations, all working as pure string transforms.
nodes.languageNotes: ReplaceModerators is the most complex operation — it surgically rewrites only the moderator entries while preserving surrounding INI structure.

nodes.name: ReplaceModerators
nodes.summary: Atomically replaces the full moderator list in the [Moderators] section with a new deduplicated set of IDs, preserving comments and all other INI sections.

nodes.name: AddModerator
nodes.summary: Inserts a moderator entry into the [Moderators] section, creating the section if it does not exist and skipping if the ID is already present.

nodes.name: BannedPlayerRow.cs
nodes.summary: Defines the BannedPlayerRow data record and the RosterPresentation static helper, which filters active roster entries, builds banned player display rows with name lookup, and determines admin status.

nodes.name: RosterPresentation
nodes.summary: Static helper that provides FilterActive, BuildBannedRows, and IsAdmin projection utilities for presenting roster and ban data in the UI.

nodes.name: IAdminListService.cs
nodes.summary: Service interface for loading, saving, and validating the admin INI file, providing a ResolveAdminIniPath helper and a Steam ID format validator.

nodes.name: IPlayerBanService.cs
nodes.summary: Service interface for player ban operations (Ban, Unban, ListBans), abstracting the underlying INI persistence from callers.

nodes.name: BackupConfidence.cs
nodes.summary: Defines the BackupConfidence result record and the BackupConfidenceCalculator static class that evaluates backup health by inspecting entry age, file completeness, and content classification.

nodes.name: BackupConfidenceCalculator
nodes.summary: Static calculator that produces a BackupConfidence score for a backup entry, classifying content completeness and formatting a human-readable age string.

nodes.name: Evaluate
nodes.summary: Computes a confidence record for a backup entry by checking staleness against a configurable threshold and classifying the entry's file contents.

nodes.name: BackupEntry.cs
nodes.summary: Record type representing metadata for a single backup snapshot, including timestamp, path, and file inventory.

nodes.name: BackupResult.cs
nodes.summary: Result record returned by backup operations, indicating success or failure with an optional error message.

nodes.name: IBackupService.cs
nodes.summary: Service interface for world backup operations, defining async methods for creating, listing, and restoring backups and resolving the backup storage root for a given server instance.

nodes.name: ConfigValidator.cs
nodes.summary: Pure static validator that checks a server instance configuration for missing required fields, invalid port numbers, port conflicts across instances, and path existence, returning a prioritized list of diagnostic messages.

nodes.name: ConfigValidator
nodes.summary: Static class with a comprehensive Validate method that produces ordered DiagnosticMessage results covering name, password, player count, ports, admin INI path, install path, and multi-instance port conflicts.
nodes.languageNotes: Follows core-pure-discipline: no IO except File.Exists/Directory.Exists checks; returns a new list each call.

nodes.name: Validate
nodes.summary: Runs the full validation suite against a server instance config, checking required fields, port ranges, cross-instance port conflicts, and path validity, and returns a sorted list of diagnostic messages.

nodes.name: IConfigValidator.cs
nodes.summary: Defines the IConfigValidator interface contract for validating server configuration files, exposing a single Validate method used by infrastructure implementations.

nodes.name: IConfigValidator
nodes.summary: Interface declaring the Validate method for server configuration validation.

nodes.name: IniDocument.cs
nodes.summary: Provides a round-trip-safe INI file parser and editor that preserves comments, blank lines, and line terminators while supporting section-scoped key-value read and write operations.
nodes.languageNotes: Preserves original line endings (CRLF vs LF) per-line by tracking terminators separately, enabling lossless round-trips even on files with mixed line endings.

nodes.name: IniDocument
nodes.summary: Core INI document model with Parse, TryGetValue, SetValue, InsertNewKey, and ToText methods that read and write INI content without destroying formatting or comments.

nodes.name: Load
nodes.summary: Parses raw INI text character-by-character into typed IniLine instances (blank, comment, section header, key-value), tracking per-line terminators for round-trip fidelity.

nodes.name: InsertNewKey
nodes.summary: Inserts a new key-value pair into a section, creating the section if it doesn't exist and placing the key at the correct position relative to existing keys.

nodes.name: IniLine.cs
nodes.summary: Defines the IniLine discriminated-union hierarchy (IniBlankLine, IniCommentLine, IniSectionLine, IniKeyValueLine) used by IniDocument to represent each line of an INI file with its rendered form.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses an abstract record base with concrete record subtypes — a C# functional-style discriminated union pattern.

nodes.name: CheckResults.cs
nodes.summary: Defines result records for individual server diagnostic checks, including pass/fail/warning outcomes and supporting detail fields.

nodes.name: DiagnosticMessage.cs
nodes.summary: Defines the DiagnosticMessage record carrying severity, category, and human-readable text for a single diagnostic finding surfaced through the diagnostics subsystem.

nodes.name: IDiagnosticsService.cs
nodes.summary: Declares the IDiagnosticsService interface with async methods for config validation, local query checks, external visibility checks, and version checks.

nodes.name: IDiagnosticsService
nodes.summary: Interface defining the full diagnostics contract: config validation, local UDP/TCP reachability, external visibility, and server version verification.

nodes.name: NetworkSetupModels.cs
nodes.summary: Contains all data models and the INetworkSetupService interface for the network setup inspection subsystem, including firewall rule status, port binding state, and environment detection results.

nodes.name: INetworkSetupService
nodes.summary: Service interface for inspecting network setup state, ensuring firewall rules are correct, and detecting the current LAN IPv4 address.

nodes.name: NetworkVerdict.cs
nodes.summary: Implements NetworkVerdictRules.Evaluate which combines firewall, environment, and port binding inspection inputs into a single structured network verdict with pass/warn/fail classification and human-readable guidance.

nodes.name: NetworkVerdictRules
nodes.summary: Pure static class that applies rule-based logic to network inspection inputs to produce a NetworkVerdict outcome.

nodes.name: Evaluate
nodes.summary: Evaluates network setup inputs against rule conditions and returns one of four verdict states: fully open, firewall only, no listener, or unknown.

nodes.name: RecommendedActions.cs
nodes.summary: Implements RecommendedActions.Build which translates network setup inspection results into a prioritized list of user-facing recommended actions, capped at a configurable maximum.

nodes.name: RecommendedActions
nodes.summary: Pure static class that converts diagnostic inputs into a ranked, capped list of RecommendedAction items covering firewall, port, and environment issues.

nodes.name: Build
nodes.summary: Generates all candidate recommended actions based on inspection inputs, then returns the top N by priority.

nodes.name: RecoveryFlows.cs
nodes.summary: Defines the RecoveryFlows catalog of structured multi-step recovery workflows (corrupt world, port conflict, missing executable, broken SteamCmd) used to guide users through diagnosed failure scenarios.

nodes.name: RecoveryFlows
nodes.summary: Static catalog class providing four named recovery flow definitions and a ForTag lookup method for retrieving a flow by its tag identifier.

nodes.name: InstallProgress.cs
nodes.summary: Defines the InstallProgress record representing a snapshot of a SteamCmd install or update operation's current progress, including step name, percentage, and status message.

nodes.name: IServerInstallStateService.cs
nodes.summary: Declares the IServerInstallStateService interface for evaluating the current install state of a server instance given its install directory.

nodes.name: IServerInstallStateService
nodes.summary: Interface with two Evaluate overloads for determining whether a server install directory is missing, outdated, or valid.

nodes.name: ISteamCmdService.cs
nodes.summary: Declares the ISteamCmdService interface with async methods for checking, installing, updating, and validating SteamCmd and the Abiotic Factor dedicated server.

nodes.name: ISteamCmdService
nodes.summary: Interface for SteamCmd lifecycle: installation detection, SteamCmd bootstrap, server install/update, and server file validation.

nodes.name: ServerInstallKind.cs
nodes.summary: Defines the ServerInstallKind enum discriminating between a clean install, an update of an existing install, and a validation pass.

nodes.name: ServerInstallState.cs
nodes.summary: Defines the ServerInstallState record capturing whether a server install directory exists, is valid, or is missing the expected executable.

nodes.name: SteamCmdDiagnostics.cs
nodes.summary: Provides pure static diagnostic helpers for interpreting SteamCmd output, detecting self-update failures and locked-file errors, and summarizing the overall failure mode for user-facing guidance.

nodes.name: SteamCmdDiagnostics
nodes.summary: Static class with pattern-matching helpers (LooksLikeSelfUpdateFailure, LooksLikeLockedFiles, Summarize) that parse SteamCmd console output to classify failure types.

nodes.name: LooksLikeSelfUpdateFailure
nodes.summary: Scans SteamCmd output for known self-update failure marker strings, returning true when found.

nodes.name: LooksLikeLockedFiles
nodes.summary: Detects locked-file errors in SteamCmd output by matching multiple access-denied and file-lock marker strings.

nodes.name: Summarize
nodes.summary: Returns a structured summary record describing the primary SteamCmd failure cause (locked files, self-update, or generic error) from raw output.

nodes.name: SteamCmdResult.cs
nodes.summary: Defines the SteamCmdResult record holding the exit code, stdout/stderr output, and elapsed time for a completed SteamCmd process invocation.

nodes.name: LegacyDataLocations.cs
nodes.summary: Provides LegacyDataLocations.Candidates which enumerates candidate legacy data root paths from AppData roaming and local directories for migration discovery.

nodes.name: LegacyDataLocations
nodes.summary: Static class that generates a list of legacy data root candidate paths based on well-known Windows AppData folder locations.

nodes.name: LegacyPathRebase.cs
nodes.summary: Implements LegacyPathRebase.ScrubStalePaths which clears outdated path fields on a ServerInstance record when the referenced directories or files no longer exist, returning a cleaned clone.

nodes.name: LegacyPathRebase
nodes.summary: Pure static class that validates and nullifies stale path references in a ServerInstance, accepting injectable filesystem predicates for testability.

nodes.name: ScrubStalePaths
nodes.summary: Returns a modified clone of a ServerInstance with InstallPath, WorldPath, SandboxIniPath, and AdminIniPath cleared when they are blank or the file/directory is missing.

nodes.name: PlatformAccessMode.cs

nodes.name: ServerInstance.cs

nodes.name: ServerInstance

nodes.name: FirewallInspectionParser.cs
nodes.summary: Parses JSON output from the firewall inspection PowerShell script into structured FirewallInspection and FirewallApplyOutcome records, extracting rule status, port bindings, and environment information.

nodes.name: FirewallInspectionParser
nodes.summary: Static parser class with ParseInspection and ParseOutcome methods that deserialize firewall script JSON output using System.Text.Json with safe property extraction helpers.

nodes.name: ParseInspection
nodes.summary: Deserializes a firewall inspection JSON document into a FirewallInspection aggregate containing rule statuses, network environment, and port binding list.

nodes.name: IPublicIpProbe.cs

nodes.name: NetworkConfidenceScore.cs

nodes.name: NetworkConfidenceScoring

nodes.name: NetworkPortValidation.cs

nodes.name: RouterChecklistBuilder.cs

nodes.name: RouterChecklistBuilder

nodes.name: ILaunchArgumentBuilder.cs

nodes.name: IServerExecutableLocator.cs

nodes.name: IServerExecutableLocator

nodes.name: IServerProcessService.cs

nodes.name: IServerProcessService

nodes.name: LaunchArgumentBuilder.cs

nodes.name: ResolveIniArg

nodes.name: LobbyCodeParser.cs

nodes.name: PlayerActivityEvent.cs

nodes.name: PlayerActivityParser.cs

nodes.name: PlayerDetailBuilder.cs

nodes.name: PlayerRosterModels.cs

nodes.name: PlayerRosterParser.cs

nodes.name: PlayerRosterTracker.cs

nodes.name: ServerHealthTracker.cs

nodes.name: ServerHealthTracker

nodes.name: StartupSequence.cs

nodes.name: publish-if-stale.ps1
nodes.summary: Claude Code stop-hook script that republishes `FacilityOverseer.exe` whenever any source file under `src/` is newer than the current published binary; publishes first to a `%LOCALAPPDATA%` staging directory to avoid OneDrive/AV file-lock failures, then copies the exe with a six-attempt retry loop.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a staging-then-copy pattern to work around OneDrive sync and antivirus locks on single-file .NET publish; always exits 0 and logs failures rather than throwing, so the Claude Code hook harness is never broken by a build error.

layers.name: Presentation Layer
layers.description: WPF UI windows, XAML views, MVVM view models, behaviors, converters, and theme resources that compose the Facility Overseer desktop application.

layers.name: Domain Layer
layers.description: Pure C# domain logic, interfaces, models, and service contracts covering server runtime, sandbox schema, networking, admin/ban management, backup, diagnostics, and world identity — free of any IO dependencies.

layers.name: Infrastructure Layer
layers.description: IO-bound implementations of Core interfaces: filesystem operations, SteamCMD integration, Windows process management, network queries, JSON persistence stores, and Windows firewall/network setup.

layers.name: Test Layer
layers.description: xUnit test suites that verify domain logic, infrastructure implementations, and integration behavior across all subsystems, maintaining the 252-test passing baseline.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project documentation including the user-facing README, agent routing manifest, architecture reference, master refactor plan, and active UI tweaks plan used to guide AI-assisted development.

layers.name: Project Configuration
layers.description: Solution-level MSBuild configuration, NuGet package version management, .NET SDK version pin, git attributes, and the PowerShell publish script that wire together the four-project solution build.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: README.md is the starting point for understanding Facility Overseer: what it does, who it is for, and the design philosophy behind it. The motto "Do not hardcode the facility. Discover it" captures the key architectural principle — the app reads game settings from metadata rather than baking in hardcoded knowledge of each option. Reading this first gives you the mental model the rest of the codebase is built around.

tour.title: Solution & Build Foundation
tour.description: The solution file and Directory.Build.props define the four-project structure — App, Core, Infrastructure, and Tests — and apply shared MSBuild rules to all of them at once. TreatWarningsAsErrors is set globally here, which means the compiler is used as a correctness gate and not just a suggestion engine. Understanding the build scaffold explains why every project in the solution shares the same nullable annotations, target framework, and single-file publish settings without repeating them.
tour.languageLesson: Directory.Build.props is an MSBuild feature that automatically applies shared properties to every project under the same directory tree. This avoids copy-pasting <Nullable>enable</Nullable> or <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors> into each .csproj file individually.

tour.title: Application Entry Point & DI Wiring
tour.description: App.xaml.cs is where the application actually starts. It bootstraps the .NET Generic Host — the same hosting model used by ASP.NET Core — so all services are registered in a single DI container and resolved by type. This file also handles the very first decision a user sees: which folder will hold the server data. If no DataRoot has been selected yet, the app prompts here before the main window ever opens.
tour.languageLesson: WPF applications can use the .NET Generic Host (Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting) to get the same IServiceCollection dependency injection, IConfiguration, and IHostedService lifecycle that ASP.NET Core apps enjoy — the App class overrides OnStartup() to build and start the host instead of just showing a window.

tour.title: Data Root & Path Split
tour.description: AppPaths.cs is the first Infrastructure service the DI host resolves. It implements the DataRoot/VolatileRoot split: durable world saves and configuration go under DataRoot (which can live anywhere the user chooses, even OneDrive), while SteamCMD and the server binaries go under VolatileRoot (a local-only path). This separation is critical on machines where cloud sync would corrupt large binary files, and it is why the app prompts for a data root before doing anything else.
tour.languageLesson: Splitting durable data from volatile binaries is a pattern common in server management tools. The OneDrive-awareness here prevents a class of hard-to-diagnose corruption bugs where a cloud sync agent locks a file mid-write while the game server is also writing to it.

tour.title: The Core Domain Model
tour.description: ServerInstance.cs is the fundamental record that every other part of the system talks about. It carries the world ID, display name, port numbers, and references to the paths computed by AppPaths. Because Core has no IO dependencies, ServerInstance is a plain immutable record that can be instantiated in tests without touching the filesystem. It is the vocabulary shared between the Presentation layer's ViewModels and the Infrastructure layer's services.
tour.languageLesson: In the App → Core ← Infrastructure architecture, Core defines interfaces and records that Infrastructure implements and App consumes. Core never references Infrastructure, so domain logic can be tested without any IO — a C# record is ideal here because its structural equality makes assertions in unit tests straightforward.

tour.title: World Path Layout
tour.description: WorldSaveLayout.cs is the central hub for all path arithmetic involving a world. It derives every relevant path — the SandboxSettings.ini location, the save folder, the staged vs. in-place config variant — from a single DataRoot and world ID. Centralizing this math means the rest of the codebase never constructs paths ad hoc; if the layout ever changes, only this one file needs to change.

tour.title: Server Installation via SteamCMD
tour.description: SteamCmdService.cs handles downloading SteamCMD on first run and then using it to install or update the Abiotic Factor dedicated server (Steam app ID 2857200) with anonymous login. This is the first thing that happens after a DataRoot is selected and before a world can be started. The service lives in Infrastructure because it shells out to an external process and writes files to disk — two operations that Core deliberately avoids.

tour.title: World Configuration: Schema-Driven Settings
tour.description: The SandboxSettings.ini configuration system is driven entirely by metadata rather than hardcoded knowledge. The setting-metadata.json file embeds the display label, type, valid range, and default value for every known INI key, and SettingMetadataCatalog.cs loads and indexes it at startup. When the game adds a new setting, the app picks it up automatically and places it on the Advanced tab — no code change required. IniDocument.cs provides the lossless round-trip parser that reads and writes the INI files without corrupting line endings or comments.
tour.languageLesson: An embedded JSON metadata catalog paired with a lossless INI parser is a "discover, don't hardcode" pattern: the app treats SandboxSettings.ini as a generic key-value store and lets the metadata drive the UI rather than writing a dedicated UI control for each setting. This keeps the app forward-compatible with game updates.

tour.title: Launching & Managing the Server Process
tour.description: ServerProcessService.cs is responsible for actually starting the Abiotic Factor server binary and keeping it alive. It uses a Win32 Job Object to bind the server process to the app's lifetime — if the manager exits unexpectedly, the server process is cleaned up automatically rather than left as an orphan. The service also streams stdout so the log panel in the UI receives live output without polling.
tour.languageLesson: Win32 Job Objects let a parent process declare that all child processes it spawns are part of the same job, and that the job should be terminated when the parent exits. This is the correct Windows-native solution to the "orphan process" problem that plagues simpler approaches using Process.Kill().

tour.title: Runtime Health State Machine
tour.description: ServerHealthTracker.cs is a pure state machine in Core that watches the stream of log lines coming from the server process and classifies them into health states: Stopped, Starting, Running, Degraded, and so on. Because it lives in Core and has no IO, it can be driven entirely by in-memory string inputs in tests — and the ServerHealthTrackerTests.cs suite does exactly that, verifying every transition. The health state is the source of truth that drives the status indicator dots in the UI.
tour.languageLesson: A pure state machine with no IO side-effects is one of the most testable patterns in C#: you feed it inputs (log line strings) and assert on outputs (health enum values) without any mocking infrastructure. This is why the tests for it are simple and fast despite the complex state transitions they cover.

tour.title: Live Player Roster & Network Verification
tour.description: PlayerRosterTracker.cs builds and maintains the live list of connected players by parsing join/leave events from server logs. A2SQueryClient.cs cross-checks those log-derived counts against the Valve A2S_INFO protocol, which queries the game server directly over UDP — a discrepancy between the two signals indicates the server has degraded. NetworkConfidenceScore.cs then aggregates the A2S result, public IP probe, and CGNAT detection into a single 0-100 score that tells the user how likely their server is actually reachable from the internet.

tour.title: Firewall Setup
tour.description: WindowsNetworkSetupService.cs manages the Windows Firewall rules required to make the server reachable on the local network and the internet. It generates PowerShell scripts that can be run once to open the game and query ports, and also provides inspection scripts to verify existing rules. This service connects the network confidence score from Step 11 back to a concrete remediation action: if the firewall is blocking traffic, the app can generate the exact script to fix it.
tour.languageLesson: Generating PowerShell scripts as strings rather than calling firewall APIs directly is a transparency pattern: the user can read and audit the exact commands before running them with elevation, rather than trusting the app to silently manipulate system policy.

tour.title: Automated World Backups
tour.description: FileBackupService.cs creates timestamped zip archives of the world save folder whenever the server stops cleanly or on a schedule. It also implements BackupConfidence scoring, which tells the UI how recent and complete the last backup was — a low-confidence score surfaces as a warning in the backup panel. This is the last Infrastructure service in the server lifecycle: install, configure, start, monitor, and finally protect the save data.

tour.title: ViewModel Layer: MainViewModel & ServerInstanceViewModel
tour.description: MainViewModel.cs is the central orchestrator that holds all world tabs and owns the server lifecycle commands (Install, Start, Stop). For each world tab it creates a ServerInstanceViewModel.cs, which binds the health state machine, the live roster, the network confidence score, admin/ban lists, backup status, and sandbox settings into a single coherent per-world data context. Together these two ViewModels translate every Core domain event and Infrastructure callback into observable properties that the XAML bindings consume.
tour.languageLesson: The MVVM (Model-View-ViewModel) pattern in WPF uses INotifyPropertyChanged to push updates from the ViewModel to the View without the View holding a reference to any business logic. MainViewModel owns the application-level state while ServerInstanceViewModel scopes state to a single world tab — a two-level ViewModel hierarchy that mirrors the UI's two-level tab structure.

tour.title: UI Layout, Theme & Converters
tour.description: MainWindow.xaml is the 1854-line XAML file that assembles the full UI: world tabs, server control panel, sandbox settings editor, network diagnostics card, player roster, backup history, and log tail — all bound to the ViewModels from Step 14. Overseer.xaml provides the global dark theme and visual language that styles every control consistently. Converters.cs bridges the gap between domain enums and visual representation: a ServerHealth.Degraded value becomes a red brush, a BackupConfidence.Stale value makes a warning badge visible.
tour.languageLesson: WPF IValueConverter implementations are stateless translation functions between ViewModel data and XAML visual properties. By centralizing all enum-to-brush mappings in Converters.cs, the theme can change the meaning of colors in one place without touching any ViewModel or XAML template that uses those converters.
